old_claim.note: Public Science Lectures provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Royal Society meeting culture began after a Gresham College astronomy lecture, making public science lectures the immediate lecture-setting predecessor.

source_shape.source_locator: The Royal Society history page says the first meeting on 28 November 1660 followed a lecture by Christopher Wren, Professor of Astronomy at Gresham College in London.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source directly places the first Royal Society meeting immediately after a Gresham College astronomy lecture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The lecture was the immediate setting that preceded the first meeting, so this is historical lineage rather than a generic operating enabler.

ontology_before: Early Scientific Conferences depended on Public Science Lectures as an unsourced generic enabler.
ontology_after: Early Scientific Conferences depend on Public Science Lectures as the Gresham College lecture lineage for the first Royal Society meeting.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: public_science_lectures remains direct but is now typed historical_predecessor with official-source evidence.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped away from Royal Society/Gresham College meeting origins.
A stronger source showed the first meeting was unrelated to a public scientific lecture setting.
Public Science Lectures were narrowed away from Gresham College-style public lecture programs.
short_reason: The first Royal Society meeting followed a Gresham College astronomy lecture.
